content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 30, 2004


Michele L. Robinson
8738 Bronson Drive
Granite Bay, CA 95746

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-04-151

Dear Ms. Robinson:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding whether the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ would restrict you from participating in certain activities outside your employment as a public official. Since you do not ask a question governed by the Act, we are only able to provide general assistance.²

The Act prohibits a public official from making or participating in the making of a governmental decision in which he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) Since your question does not address a governmental decision, we are unable to analyze your facts further. However, we suggest you consult your agency's statement of incompatible activities, if applicable, and contact the Attorney General's office at (916) 324-5765 to discuss whether Government Code section 1090 may apply. We have included a document entitled, "Can I Vote?" and an advice letter from our office to provide you with general information about the Act and outside income.




_____________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Since you have not posed a question relating to the Act, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to regulation 18329(c)(copy enclosed).²

File No. I-04-151
Page No. 2

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division

Enclosures

E:\AdviceLtrs\04-151

sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide specific analysis because the requester's question does not address a governmental decision as defined by the Act. The FPPC recommends consulting the agency's statement of incompatible activities and the Attorney General's office regarding Government Code section 1090.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; this is an informal assistance response (I-04-151) indicating the request was outside FPPC's purview. Letter provides general guidance and referrals instead of specific advice.

QUESTION: Whether the Political Reform Act restricts a public official from participating in certain activities outside their employment.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC cannot provide specific analysis because the requester's question does not address a governmental decision as defined by the Act. The FPPC recommends consulting the agency's statement of incompatible activities and the Attorney General's office regarding Government Code section 1090.
